postText:
Bangalore is running out of water. Not someday. Now.

The city sits on a plateau with no major river running through it. That was always going to be a problem. But the groundwater is also going. Borewells that hit water at 200 feet now need to go to 1,500. And still sometimes come up dry.

What gets me is how much of this was predictable. The lakes were encroached. The wetlands were built over. Every recharge point the city had, someone put a building on it.

The Cauvery only covers about half the city's actual demand. The rest runs on borrowed water and borrowed time.

I built FloodReady for Delhi because excess water destroys lives. But the opposite problem is just as bad and somehow gets less attention. Too much water is a disaster. Too little water is a slow one.

Both are policy failures. Both were visible years before they hit.

What would actually fix this, in your view? Because I'm not sure anyone has a real answer yet.

#UrbanPolicy #CivicTech #PublicPolicy
